name: Catch website-first pilot launch
successDefinition: Catch is publicly operating in Mumbai and Indore with accurate market claims, reachable legal and help pages, production website monitoring, and a reviewed supply pipeline ready for the first 50 organizers plus their events.

statusDefinitions.todo: Unclaimed work that can start when its dependencies are met.
statusDefinitions.in_progress: Actively owned work with a current next action.
statusDefinitions.blocked: Cannot proceed without a named decision, credential, or external state change.
statusDefinitions.ready: Acceptance criteria are met and final publication or owner approval remains.
statusDefinitions.done: Acceptance criteria are met and durable evidence is linked.
statusDefinitions.deferred: Intentionally excluded until its revisit condition is true.

issueStatusDefinitions.open: Confirmed defect or readiness gap that has not been claimed.
issueStatusDefinitions.in_progress: A named owner is actively implementing or externally configuring the fix.
issueStatusDefinitions.fixed: The fix is merged and any production-facing behavior is verified live.
issueStatusDefinitions.deferred: The issue is deliberately outside the current launch tranche with a revisit condition.

decisions.decision: Launch only Mumbai and Indore as live markets for the pilot.
decisions.rationale: Concentrate supply and operating learning before expanding city claims.

decisions.decision: Keep every other configured city on the waitlist until the week of 2026-07-27 and at least three documented end-to-end pilot test runs pass.
decisions.rationale: Expansion follows observed pilot behavior rather than an untested nationwide claim.

decisions.decision: Launch the website aggressively before the native apps.
decisions.rationale: Public discovery and host acquisition can begin while store-launch blockers remain.

decisions.decision: Defer paid-event launch work from this tranche.
decisions.rationale: Payments have separate unresolved readiness gates.

decisions.decision: The next supply target is 50 organizers plus their events across the Mumbai and Indore pilot markets.
decisions.rationale: Organizer coverage is the primary supply goal; event records attach to those organizers and do not reduce the organizer count.

blockers.summary: Resolved: Torana; suvrat@catchdates.com; 65, Manish Puri, Indore, Madhya Pradesh, India 452018; Indore, Madhya Pradesh courts; Suvrat Garg, Founder.
Resolved: GTM-K7KLNQXP is published as Version 2 with Google tag G-CH7WMQY5FV and is the enforced production deployment default.
Resolved: publish 50 organizers across Mumbai and Indore, plus the events belonging to those organizers.

issues.summary: Resolved: deployable organizer artifacts now enforce the Mumbai and Indore pilot market set; Delhi and Bhag Club are absent from production routes, discovery, and sitemap output.
issues.nextAction: Keep the generator and promotion-bridge market guards required as new organizer batches are reviewed.
evidence: Marketing Website workflow 29934872745 and Deploy marketing Hosting completed successfully
2026-07-22 live probe: /organizers/bhag/ returned 404; directory and sitemap contained neither Bhag nor Delhi; /organizers/afterfly/ and its sitemap entry returned 200

issues.summary: Resolved: GTM Version 3 forwards an explicit allowlist of consented waitlist, host-lead, host-application, organizer-claim, CTA, and client-error dataLayer events to GA4.
issues.nextAction: Retain the allowlist and exact-once Preview evidence; add GA4 Realtime evidence when Catch property access is restored.
evidence: GTM-K7KLNQXP Version 3: Website production observability v1, published 2026-07-22 20:50 IST
Tag Assistant Preview: cta_click produced Catch website custom events exactly once
Trigger Catch website observability events uses a six-event anchored regex allowlist
2026-07-22 GA4 Realtime confirmed six page_view events and three production page titles

issues.summary: Resolved: source analytics remove query strings and GTM Version 3 overrides GA4 page_location with scheme, hostname, and pathname only.
issues.nextAction: Keep query strings and request identifiers out of analytics payloads as new events are added.
evidence: GTM-K7KLNQXP Version 3 Catch production GA4 page_location = https://{{Page Hostname}}{{Page Path}}

issues.summary: Resolved: Supply Intake filters organizer packets by requested market and fails stale, expired, future-dated, or market-mismatched event bridges.
issues.nextAction: Use the enforced failures while generating current reviewed Mumbai and Indore bridges.
evidence: 2026-07-22 read-only Mumbai shadow audit: 18 items, 7 expired events

issues.summary: No current Indore event-intake bridge exists, and the available Mumbai bridge is a stale June sample that correctly fails the live publisher check.
issues.nextAction: Capture and review current Mumbai and Indore source results, candidates, and decisions, then generate two current live-checkable bridges.

issues.summary: Reviewed external-event capture and import planning stop at a no-write plan; no production writer or publication authority implementation exists yet.
issues.nextAction: Implement the accepted read-only external-event authority and reviewed writer with market identity, idempotency, takedown, and dry-run receipts before publishing organizer events.

issues.summary: New search candidates cannot become schema-complete OrganizerEntity records through one governed command; the first batch requires a manual reviewed batch artifact.
issues.nextAction: Author the first 10-organizer batch manually, then add a reviewed candidate-to-entity scaffolding command after the pilot proves the required fields.

issues.summary: The Event Intake publisher owns only eventIntakeDashboards/current, so publishing Indore would replace Mumbai instead of preserving both pilot markets.
issues.nextAction: Move the dashboard projection to market-scoped documents and update the admin reader before publishing two city dashboards.

issues.summary: Supply acquisition is not autonomous: Operations disables network/model access, event search needs a paid key for automation, and organizer search capture is manual.
issues.nextAction: Use the free manual browser capture path for the first 10 organizers; decide on an acquisition provider only after measuring pilot throughput.

issues.summary: Resolved: lockfile-only upgrades moved fast-uri to 3.1.4 and protobufjs to 7.6.5, leaving the production dependency audit at zero known vulnerabilities.
issues.nextAction: Retain npm audit in the release loop and triage any new production advisory before publication.

issues.summary: The website provides no visible way to revisit or withdraw a stored analytics consent choice.
issues.nextAction: Add a footer privacy-settings control and automated essential-only network proof after the launch-critical event pipeline is verified.

issues.summary: The signed-in Google Analytics account picker exposes only the unrelated Momotogo property, so Catch custom-event delivery cannot yet be captured independently in GA4 Realtime or DebugView.
issues.nextAction: Grant suvrat.garg@gmail.com access to the Catch GA4 property for G-CH7WMQY5FV or provide the property URL, then capture cta_click in Realtime or DebugView.
evidence: 2026-07-22 Google Analytics universal picker showed account Suvrat Garg 155931484 with only Momotogo GA4 property 314503948
Tag Assistant independently proved GTM-K7KLNQXP and G-CH7WMQY5FV connected and cta_click fired once

tasks.title: Align every public market claim to Mumbai and Indore only
acceptance: Mumbai and Indore are the only live website markets.
Delhi, Bangalore, Pune, and Hyderabad are presented as waitlist markets.
Market-pack and website route/build checks pass.
Production pages show the two-city claim after deployment.
tasks.nextAction: Preserve the two-city guard until DEC-002 expansion conditions are explicitly met.
evidence: 2026-07-22 live probe: / and /organizers/ returned HTTP 200 with pilot markers
Marketing Website workflow 29934872745 and Deploy marketing Hosting completed successfully
2026-07-22 live probe: /organizers/bhag/ returned 404; Bhag and Delhi were absent from /organizers/ and /sitemap.xml; AFTER FLY Indore remained live

tasks.title: Publish privacy, terms, and help pages
acceptance: /privacy/, /terms/, and /help/ return HTTP 200 with route-specific canonical metadata.
Footer links and the public contact destination are reachable.
Privacy rights, correction/deletion, grievance handling, 18+ eligibility, public-source listings, claim/correction/takedown, safety, and external-event boundaries are explained plainly.
Owner facts are confirmed and no placeholder text is published.
tasks.nextAction: Route legal-policy changes through founder and counsel review before republishing.

tasks.title: Monitor production website availability and launch-critical content
acceptance: A free scheduled workflow probes launch-critical public routes.
The probe fails on wrong status, missing canonical metadata, or missing launch markers.
A successful production workflow run is linked as evidence.
tasks.nextAction: Review scheduled failures and keep pilot markers current as supply is published.
evidence: 2026-07-22 independent production probe passed all five launch-critical routes

tasks.title: Deliver consented website traffic, conversion, and client-error events to GTM/GA4
acceptance: prod-hosting has a confirmed VITE_GTM_ID.
A consented production session produces page_view and launch conversion events in GA4 Realtime or DebugView.
No analytics or marketing tag loads before consent.
Evidence is linked without exposing credentials.
tasks.nextAction: Restore access to the Catch GA4 property and capture the already-tested cta_click event in Realtime or DebugView to close the last acceptance item.
evidence: GTM-K7KLNQXP Version 3: Website production observability v1
Tag Assistant Preview: Catch website custom events fired exactly once for cta_click
Deployed site-DX9pXazw.js contains GTM-K7KLNQXP, googletagmanager, gtm.js, and dataLayer
2026-07-22 GA4 Realtime confirmed six page_view events across Home, Organizer directory, and AFTER FLY

tasks.title: Complete three documented Mumbai/Indore end-to-end test runs
acceptance: Three runs cover discovery, organizer detail, external registration or claim, correction/help contact, and monitoring response.
Each run records city, timestamp, result, defects, and follow-up owner.
No unresolved P0 trust, safety, or availability defect remains.
tasks.nextAction: Unblock after one reviewed organizer exists in each pilot city and the custom analytics pipeline is verified.

tasks.title: Research and review the first 10 organizers: five Mumbai and five Indore
acceptance: Five Mumbai and five Indore organizer candidates have source URLs and capture dates.
Each candidate is deduplicated and represented by a schema-complete organizer batch record with crawl disabled by default.
Publication packets are generated and reviewed before any website projection is approved.
At least one organizer per city is deployable for the city test runs.
tasks.nextAction: Use the 101-query search plan and manual browser capture path to build a review packet; do not publish from search results directly.
evidence: operations/launch/pilot_supply_shortlist.json: 5 Mumbai + 5 Indore, 6 review-now and 4 organizer-only pending event verification

tasks.title: Research, review, and publish 50 pilot organizers plus their events
acceptance: Exactly 50 organizer records are approved across Mumbai and Indore; attached event records do not reduce that count.
Every organizer and event record has source provenance, freshness, city, claim state, and a human-reviewed publication decision.
Unclaimed listings expose correction, claim, and takedown paths.
Production count and sampled page checks match the approved publication receipt.
tasks.nextAction: After the three pilot runs pass, run the existing supply-intake workflow in review-first mode for 50 organizers and their events.

tasks.title: Launch consumer and host apps publicly
tasks.nextAction: Revisit after the website pilot and remaining store blockers are explicitly cleared.

tasks.title: Enable paid events
tasks.nextAction: Revisit only after payment, refund, settlement, and legal readiness are separately approved.
